title: NSX-T Transport Node Overlay Path Health
spl:
index=vmware sourcetype="vmware:nsx:tn_diag" earliest=-24h
| where pkt_loss_pct>1 OR mtu_issue=1
| stats max(pkt_loss_pct) as max_loss by src_tn, dst_tn
| sort -max_loss
| head 50
description: Validates GENEVE overlay between TNs (packet loss, MTU) beyond simple UP/DOWN (UC-18.2.5).
value: Validates GENEVE overlay between TNs (packet loss, MTU) beyond simple UP/DOWN (UC-18.2.5).
implementation: Ingest Traceflow or TN diagnostic jobs on schedule. Alert on loss >1% between any TN pair in same pool.
visualization: Heatmap (TN × TN loss), Table (worst pairs), Line chart (loss trend).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `vmware_nsx_addon`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `vmware:nsx:tn_diag`, traceflow results.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest Traceflow or TN diagnostic jobs on schedule. Alert on loss >1% between any TN pair in same pool.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vmware sourcetype="vmware:nsx:tn_diag" earliest=-24h
| where pkt_loss_pct>1 OR mtu_issue=1
| stats max(pkt_loss_pct) as max_loss by src_tn, dst_tn
| sort -max_loss
| head 50
```

Understanding this SPL

**NSX-T Transport Node Overlay Path Health** — Validates GENEVE overlay between TNs (packet loss, MTU) beyond simple UP/DOWN (UC-18.2.5).

Documented **Data sources**: `vmware:nsx:tn_diag`, traceflow results. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `vmware_nsx_addon`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vmware; **sourcetype**: vmware:nsx:tn_diag. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vmware, sourcetype="vmware:nsx:tn_diag", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where pkt_loss_pct>1 OR mtu_issue=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by src_tn, dst_tn** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Limits the number of rows with `head`.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with VMware NSX Manager (monitoring, alarms, and relevant inventory) for the same time window.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Heatmap (TN × TN loss), Table (worst pairs), Line chart (loss trend).
grandmaExplanation: We check GENEVE overlay between TNs (packet loss, MTU) beyond simple UP/DOWN (UC-18.2.5) — so traffic problems, routing mistakes, and risky changes surface fast.
knownFalsePositives: VTEP issues during link maintenance, port channel rebalancing, or planned fabric expansion.
